headline: Building Energy Code Compliance Below 25% Despite Mandate
context: Malaysian Standard for Energy Efficiency achieved only 25% compliance despite being mandatory since 2019.

cards.big: Only 25% of new non-residential buildings comply with energy code
cards.sub: Mandatory since 2019 but weak enforcement leaves buildings among Asia least efficient

cards.big: Non-compliant buildings consume 40% more energy than code allows
cards.sub: Excess energy costs RM8.5 billion annually in unnecessary electricity

cards.big: Local authorities issued only 180 non-compliance notices in 6 years
cards.sub: Building plans routinely waive energy requirements without documentation

cards.big: Singapore achieves 89% compliance through integrated plan approval
cards.sub: Key difference is Singapore rejects plans that fail energy requirements

cards.big: Energy code failure tests whether mandatory standards are meaningless without enforcement
Below 25% energy code compliance despite legal mandate. Codes without enforcement are architecture fiction.
